_comment: All 4 locale files (en, ru, uk, es) MUST have IDENTICAL key structure. Use this as the canonical key tree. Values are placeholders showing required structure.

hero.h1: USCIS help for Ukrainians in the U.S.
hero.subtitle: Official sources, document guidance, and clear next steps for U4U, re-parole, TPS, EAD, I-94, and case tracking.
hero.trustStrip: Official-source based · 4 languages · Not a law firm

caseStatus.title: Check your USCIS case status
caseStatus.subtitle: Enter your 13-character receipt number. We don't store it.
caseStatus.errorInvalid: Receipt number format is invalid. It should be 3 letters (EAC, WAC, LIN, SRC, NBC, MSC, IOE) followed by 10 digits.
caseStatus.disclaimer: We don't store your receipt number. Submitting opens the official USCIS Case Status page in a new tab.

uscisCaseStatus.title: USCIS Case Status
uscisCaseStatus.description: Track your case with your receipt number

cbpI94.title: CBP I-94
cbpI94.description: Find your most recent I-94 record

services.sectionTitle: What do you need help with?

parole-expires-soon.title: Parole expires soon
parole-expires-soon.shortProblem: Your parole expires in days or weeks

biometrics.title: Biometrics appointment
biometrics.shortProblem: What to expect, how to reschedule

rfe-denial.title: RFE or denial
rfe-denial.shortProblem: Request for Evidence or denial response

official-sources.title: Official sources
official-sources.shortProblem: All official USCIS, CBP, DOJ links

servicePages._pattern: Each slug below has 4 sub-keys: title, subtitle, whatHelps (array of 3-5 strings), commonMistakes (array of 3 strings), officialNote (1-2 sentences)

parole-expires-soon.title: Your parole is expiring soon
parole-expires-soon.subtitle: Understand what you have, what you need, and where to verify the official rules.
whatHelps: Knowing the difference between parole expiration and case receipt
Identifying which form to file (typically I-131)
Locating your I-94 and parole travel authorization documents
Understanding state of presence vs work authorization
commonMistakes: Believing a receipt notice automatically extends parole
Filing only the form without personal evidence
Confusing parole status with EAD validity
parole-expires-soon.officialNote: USCIS publishes Uniting for Ukraine guidance on its official website. Always verify dates and requirements directly from USCIS.

re-parole-u4u.title: How to file for re-parole (U4U)
re-parole-u4u.subtitle: What documents to gather, what evidence is now expected, and where to find official requirements.
whatHelps: Gathering supporting documents required by USCIS
Understanding personal vs general justification
Reviewing the Form I-131 and instructions
Knowing the difference between re-parole and EAD applications
commonMistakes: Assuming general country conditions are sufficient
Filing without recent personal evidence (medical, family, employment)
Forgetting to include the Travel Authorization document
re-parole-u4u.officialNote: Form I-131 and instructions are available on the USCIS website. The instructions are the authoritative source for what evidence is required.

tps-ukraine.title: TPS Ukraine
tps-ukraine.subtitle: Designation period, EAD auto-extension dates, and re-registration windows.
whatHelps: Confirming whether you registered in time
Understanding the TPS designation period vs EAD validity
Locating your EAD card category code
Reviewing re-registration windows on USCIS
commonMistakes: Assuming TPS designation extension automatically extends your EAD
Missing re-registration windows
Working past your EAD auto-extension cutoff
tps-ukraine.officialNote: USCIS posts current TPS Ukraine designation dates and auto-extension EAD dates on its official TPS country page. Federal Register notices are the authoritative source.

ead-work-permit.title: EAD (Work permit)
ead-work-permit.subtitle: How USCIS treats EAD applications, categories, and what an EAD covers.
whatHelps: Identifying your correct EAD category
Reviewing Form I-765 instructions
Understanding fee waivers via Form I-912
Knowing the difference between auto-extension and new approval
commonMistakes: Filing under the wrong category code
Filing for work authorization on a re-parole receipt alone
Missing the I-9 reverification deadline with employer
ead-work-permit.officialNote: Form I-765 and the Federal Register are the authoritative sources for EAD eligibility, categories, and auto-extension dates.

i-94.title: I-94 record
i-94.subtitle: How to retrieve, verify, and request corrections to your I-94 with CBP.
whatHelps: Retrieving your most recent I-94 from CBP
Understanding class of admission codes
Identifying when an I-94 correction request is needed
commonMistakes: Confusing I-94 expiration with parole expiration
Not retrieving I-94 before applying for benefits
Submitting incorrect class of admission on forms
i-94.officialNote: CBP I-94 records are retrieved at i94.cbp.dhs.gov. CBP, not USCIS, issues and corrects I-94 records.

uscis-case-status.title: Check your USCIS case status
uscis-case-status.subtitle: Use your receipt number to track your case on USCIS.
whatHelps: Locating your receipt number on USCIS notices
Understanding what each status step means
Knowing the difference between processing time and your case status
commonMistakes: Entering the wrong receipt number format
Confusing biometrics scheduling with case approval
Calling USCIS before checking online status
uscis-case-status.officialNote: USCIS case status is tracked at egov.uscis.gov. Receipt number formats begin with EAC, WAC, LIN, SRC, NBC, MSC, or IOE followed by 10 digits.

payment-problem.title: USCIS payment problem
payment-problem.subtitle: What to do when your payment processed but USCIS shows your case as unpaid.
whatHelps: Confirming the charge on your bank statement
Locating your payment confirmation in your USCIS online account
Understanding when to contact USCIS Contact Center
commonMistakes: Paying again before confirming the original payment status
Not saving the bank statement showing the charge
Waiting too long before contacting USCIS
payment-problem.officialNote: USCIS payment issues are handled through your USCIS online account at my.uscis.gov.

biometrics.title: Biometrics appointment
biometrics.subtitle: What biometrics involves, what to bring, and how to reschedule.
whatHelps: Bringing required identification documents
Knowing what biometrics involves (fingerprints, photo, signature)
Understanding rescheduling rules
commonMistakes: Missing the appointment without rescheduling
Bringing wrong identification documents
Assuming biometrics is the same as case approval
biometrics.officialNote: USCIS lists biometrics services and fees on its official website.

rfe-denial.title: Responding to an RFE or denial
rfe-denial.subtitle: What an RFE means, deadlines, and where to find official guidance.
whatHelps: Reading the RFE letter to identify exactly what is requested
Identifying the response deadline
Understanding when a Motion to Reopen vs an appeal is appropriate
commonMistakes: Missing the RFE deadline
Sending only some of the requested evidence
Confusing an RFE with a denial
rfe-denial.officialNote: USCIS Policy Manual is the authoritative reference for RFE and denial procedures.

translate-document.title: Translate a document for USCIS
translate-document.subtitle: Translation requirements under 8 CFR 103.2(b)(3).
whatHelps: Understanding USCIS translation certification requirements
Knowing which documents commonly need translation
Identifying who can certify a translation
commonMistakes: Submitting an untranslated foreign-language document
Using a translation without a certification of competence
Confusing notarization with translation certification
translate-document.officialNote: USCIS translation requirements are codified at 8 CFR 103.2(b)(3) (Code of Federal Regulations).

form-draft-helper.title: Which form do I need?
form-draft-helper.subtitle: Common immigration scenarios and the USCIS forms typically associated with them.
whatHelps: Identifying the topic that matches your situation
Locating the official USCIS form page
Reading form instructions before starting
commonMistakes: Filing a similar but wrong form
Using an outdated form edition
Skipping the official instructions
form-draft-helper.officialNote: All USCIS forms are at uscis.gov/forms. Form instructions are part of the official form.

official-sources.title: Official sources
official-sources.subtitle: All authoritative US government sources we link to.
whatHelps: USCIS for forms, fees, and policy
CBP for I-94 records
DOJ EOIR for accredited representatives
Federal Register for designation notices
commonMistakes: Trusting unofficial websites for processing times
Following advice from social media without verification
Using outdated form editions
official-sources.officialNote: Always verify any guidance you receive against the official source listed for that topic.

translatePage.safeStatement: USCIS generally requires a full English translation and translator certification for foreign-language documents submitted to USCIS. This page does not create a certified translation.

askQuestion.title: Don't see your question?
askQuestion.subtitle: Browse our knowledge base or contact us.

card1.title: Find official sources
card1.description: Every topic links to the authoritative USCIS, CBP, or DOJ source.

card2.title: Organize your documents
card2.description: Know what to gather before you file.

card3.title: Get clear next steps
card3.description: Understand what to do — and what not to do.

documentTools.footnote: Document tools are coming soon. These pages explain what to prepare and link to official requirements.

telegramStrip.title: Community access
telegramStrip.fallback: Telegram channel and bot will be added after verification.

miaWidget.header: Hi! I'm Mia, your guide to Messenginfo.
miaWidget.footer: I'm a quick navigator, not a chat assistant.

disclaimer.title: Important
disclaimer.bold: Messenginfo is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.
disclaimer.body: We provide official-source information and self-help guidance. For legal advice, consult a licensed immigration attorney or DOJ-accredited representative.
